entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal grain crop (Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench) studied for variety attributes and farmer preferences in Tanzania

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: The country where the study on sorghum farmers' preferences was conducted

entities.name: WILLINGNESS TO PAY
entities.description: The maximum monetary amount farmers are willing to pay for specific traits of sorghum varieties

entities.name: CHOICE EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A research method used to estimate farmers' preferences and willingness to pay for variety attributes

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSES
entities.description: External environmental factors that negatively impact the growth and yield of sorghum

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The characteristic of producing a large quantity of grain per unit area

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The characteristic of reaching the harvest stage in a shorter period

entities.name: GRAIN PRICE
entities.description: The market value obtained by farmers for the harvested sorghum grain

entities.name: GRAIN COLOR
entities.description: The physical color of the sorghum grain, specifically white

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a sorghum variety to maintain productivity despite environmental stresses

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output, which variety development aims to improve

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD WELFARE
entities.description: The overall economic and social well-being of smallholder farming households

entities.name: VARIETY DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of breeding and developing new crop varieties based on farmer demand
